danger_signals.visual_form: Blue flea sprites scattered throughout levels
danger_signals.meaning: Active infestation threat; player must eliminate all fleas in a level to proceed and obtain the flag

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, distinct enemy sprites (Bobbins Brothers, Rockyfella, Flea Queen)
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring pattern recognition and strategic combat; victory unlocks save checkpoints

danger_signals.visual_form: Plok without detached arms/legs; reduced sprite mobility
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability state; loss of primary attack ability; reduced control (bouncing locomotion only)

danger_signals.visual_form: Health bar reduction (implied by Magic Fruits and Golden Fruits as restoration mechanics)
danger_signals.meaning: Approaching danger threshold; need to find healing items

safety_signals.visual_form: Flag ascending and levitating to its pole after final flea defeated
safety_signals.meaning: Level completion; safe progression point; signals readiness to advance

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible fruit objects in levels; visual growth when repeatedly struck
safety_signals.meaning: Healing resource; larger fruits indicate greater health restoration; safety/recovery opportunity

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible ammunition items scattered in levels
safety_signals.meaning: Resource accumulation for secondary attack power (amulet conversion); strategic advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Costume/vehicle transformation items with distinct visual character designs
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary enhanced abilities and invulnerability; tactical advantage against threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Checkpoint markers triggered by collecting P-L-O-K tokens (one per level without losing lives)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe resume points; progression insurance; player competence validation

faction_indicators.color_identity: Hood-headed protagonist with standard coloring (implied bright/distinctive from context)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Big square flag on rooftop; P-L-O-K token letters
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Costume variations (Plocky/boxer, Vigilante/flamethrower, Squire/musketeer, Cowboy, Rocket Plok, futuristic vehicles in Fleapit)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Plok's House (starting location); flagpoles throughout Akrillic island; Cotton Island as secondary territory

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss-tier enemy sprites; distinct from regular fleas
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cotton Island (initial theft location); confrontation point in early game progression

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Flea sprites; Flea Queen as central antagonist hidden beneath island
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Recurring flea enemies in most levels; final flea in each level drops flag (visual distinction); Flea Queen as ultimate boss
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fleapit (underground flea stronghold); island-wide infestation across Akrillic; subsurface origin
Mid-game boss location; defeat unlocks second permanent continue position

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Missing flag from Plok's rooftop; flag visible on Cotton Island (distant visual cue); fake flags encountered (underpants, striped scarf) as environmental story elements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Plok actively searches; journey to Cotton Island initiates; player must navigate foreign territory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Blue flea enemies saturate most level environments; flagpoles remain empty until all fleas eliminated; visual density of flea sprites indicates severity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mandatory flea elimination requirement in most levels; final flea behavior differs (drops flag); infestation blocks progression until cleared

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinct boss sprites (Bobbins Brothers, Rockyfella, Flea Queen); larger or visually prominent enemy designs; environmental design changes around boss encounters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss pattern attacks; defeat unlocks advancement and save mechanics; signifies power struggles for island control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinct tileset for Fleapit; futuristic vehicles and high-tech present power-ups; visual departure from natural island aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vehicle-based gameplay mechanics; advanced enemy types; final confrontation with Flea Queen in subterranean setting

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Limb hangers displaying sacrificed arms/legs on switches; Plok sprite without detached limbs; reduced animation frames for bouncing locomotion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Movement penalty (harder control via bouncing); loss of primary attack projectile function; strategic trade-off for puzzle progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Normal vs. Child's Play difficulty affects enemy sprite speeds and health bar visual representations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced enemy speed and hit points in Child's Play mode; removal of harder stages from progression path

environmental_storytelling.example: Stolen flag establishes narrative conflict; fake flags (underpants, striped scarf) encountered during search add humor and world personality while extending narrative tension

Journey from Plok's House → Cotton Island → Akrillic mainland → Fleapit creates geographic narrative arc; each location represents escalation (theft recovery → infestation discovery → underground confrontation)
Blue flea sprite concentration increases as player progresses; visual saturation communicates worsening infestation severity before explicit narrative explanation
Present costumes reference movie archetypes (Terminator, western gunslinger, deerstalker detective, boxer); Fleapit vehicles shift to sci-fi aesthetic, signaling underground technological culture
Empty flagpole = incomplete level; flag ascension = completion signal; visual clarity on objective state without text requirement
P-L-O-K tokens accumulated across levels represent player mastery; visible as collectable letters; reward system communicated through visual progression

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Drops flag that levitates to flagpole; unique behavior distinguishes it from regular fleas; signals level completion
Steal flag at game start; function as early-game boss encounter; defeat triggers first permanent continue checkpoint
Boss encounter mid-progression; defeat unlocks second permanent continue position; controls access to later game areas
Final boss in Fleapit; orchestrates infestation across island; hidden beneath surface suggests strategic positioning; ultimate confrontation required
Bouncing locomotion replaces normal movement; harder control; reduced offensive capability; hangers display sacrificed limbs on switches
Distinct visual form; enhanced attack power or special abilities (boxing, flamethrower, blunderbuss, firearms, rockets); temporary invulnerability period implied by transformation mechanic
Spawn throughout most levels; player must eliminate all before flag drops; behavior varies by difficulty (speed/health reduction in Child's Play mode)

notes: Plok! uses color identity (blue fleas), environmental density, and objective markers (flagpoles) as primary world-state communication. The game lacks explicit faction dialog or political text; instead, visual design and enemy placement convey conflict. The flag as central object communicates both narrative (stolen property) and mechanical (level completion). Costume/vehicle power-ups function as visual storytelling, establishing Plok's character through movie-inspired aesthetics. Limb sacrifice mechanics create visual vulnerability states that communicate mechanical consequence through sprite changes. Island geography (Akrillic → Cotton Island → Fleapit) structures narrative through location hierarchy. Difficulty modes adjust visual manifestation (enemy speed/health) rather than changing world-state narrative. The Flea Queen's subsurface positioning suggests underground power challenge to surface authority (Plok's kingdom).
